{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_be874c8542954fabb7b48e42","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# NEEDLE Implementation Plan\n2\t\n3\t> **N**avigates **E**very **E**nqueued **D**eliverable, **L**ogs **E**ffort\n4\t\n5\t## Design Principles\n6\t\n7\tThese six principles are non-negotiable. Every design decision in this plan traces back to one or more of them.\n8\t\n9\t1. **Deterministic order.** Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same bead ordering. There is no randomness in selection. Ties are broken by creation time.\n10\t\n11\t2. **Explicit outcome paths.** Every possible result of every operation has a named handler. If an outcome can happen, it has a handler. If it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen. The type system enforces exhaustiveness.\n12\t\n13\t3. **Platform and model agnostic.** NEEDLE wraps any headless CLI that accepts a prompt and exits. It runs on any POSIX system. It does not depend on any specific AI provider, model, or API.\n14\t\n15\t4. **Observable by default.** Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits structured telemetry. A silent worker is a broken worker. Telemetry is structured from origin (JSONL) and exportable as OpenTelemetry (OTLP) so any compliant backend — Tempo, Jaeger, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog, FABRIC — can consume NEEDLE's signals without a custom adapter.\n16\t\n17\t5. **Self-healing.** Workers detect and recover from stuck states, stale claims, crashed peers, and corrupted databases without human intervention. Recovery paths are explicit, not heuristic.\n18\t\n19\t6. **Separation of concerns.** The orchestrator does not execute work. The agent does not manage state. The bead store does not enforce workflow. Each component has one job.\n20\t\n21\t---\n22\t\n23\t## Architecture Overview\n24\t\n25\tNEEDLE is composed of five layers:\n26\t\n27\t```\n28\t┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n29\t│                        CLI Layer                              │\n30\t│  needle run | stop | list | attach | status | config          │\n31\t├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n32\t│                     Worker Layer                              │\n33\t│  Worker loop, strand waterfall, session management            │\n34\t├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n35\t│                  Coordination Layer                            │\n36\t│  Claiming, locking, heartbeats, peer awareness                │\n37\t├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n38\t│                    Agent Layer                                 │\n39\t│  Adapter interface, dispatch, result capture                  │\n40\t├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n41\t│                   Foundation Layer                             │\n42\t│  Telemetry, configuration, bead store interface, self-healing │\n43\t└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n44\t```\n45\t\n46\t### Component Map\n47\t\n48\t| Component | Responsibility | Inputs | Outputs |\n49\t|-----------|---------------|--------|---------|\n50\t| **CLI** | Parse commands, manage sessions | User commands | Worker processes |\n51\t| **Worker** | Execute the strand waterfall loop | Bead queue state | Dispatch requests, state transitions |\n52\t| **StrandRunner** | Evaluate strands in sequence | Queue state, config | Next bead or escalation |\n53\t| **Claimer** | Atomic bead claiming with serialization | Candidate bead ID | Claimed bead or race-lost signal |\n54\t| **PromptBuilder** | Construct deterministic prompts from bead context | Claimed bead | Prompt string |\n55\t| **Dispatcher** | Load adapter, render template, execute agent | Prompt, adapter config | Agent process handle |\n56\t| **OutcomeHandler** | Route exit code to explicit handler | Exit code, stdout/stderr | State transition |\n57\t| **Telemetry** | Structured event emission | Any component event | JSONL records |\n58\t| **HealthMonitor** | Heartbeat, stuck detection, peer awareness | Worker state | Recovery actions |\n59\t| **ConfigLoader** | Hierarchical config resolution | Files, env, CLI args | Resolved config |\n60\t| **BeadStore** | Abstract interface to bead backend | CRUD operations | Bead records |\n61\t| **Mitosis** | Split multi-task beads into children with dedup | Failed bead, parent's existing children | Child beads or no-op |\n62\t\n63\t---\n64\t\n65\t## Language Decision\n66\t\n67\tThe implementation language must provide:\n68\t\n69\t| Requirement | Why | Source |\n70\t|-------------|-----|--------|\n71\t| Exhaustive pattern matching | Every outcome must be handled; compiler enforces it | Principle 2, bead-lifecycle-bugs.md |\n72\t| Real module system | 45K single-file bash was unmaintainable | bash-at-scale-problems.md |\n73\t| Structured error types | Silent failures caused cascading bugs | claim-race-conditions.md |\n74\t| Native JSON support | Fragile jq parsing corrupted state | worker-starvation-lessons.md |\n75\t| Proper concurrency primitives | flock/trap/PID files were inadequate | concurrency-approaches-compared.md |\n76\t| Single binary distribution | NEEDLE must be trivially installable | Principle 3 |\n77\t| Cross-platform | Linux, macOS at minimum | Principle 3 |\n78\t| Static analysis | Catch undefined functions, unused variables at compile time | bundler-build-integrity.md |\n79\t\n80\t**Recommended: Rust.** Exhaustive `match`, `Result<T, E>` error handling, `serde_json`, `tokio` for async, single binary via static linking, cross-compilation. The beads ecosystem already has Rust precedent (beads_rust, beads-polis).\n81\t\n82\t**Acceptable alternative: Go.** Simpler learning curve, good concurrency, single binary. Lacks exhaustive matching (requires discipline instead of compiler enforcement).\n83\t\n84\t**Not acceptable: Bash, Python, Node.** Bash failed at scale (documented). Python/Node require runtime dependencies, violating single-binary distribution.\n85\t\n86\t---\n87\t\n88\t## Key Decisions from Research\n89\t\n90\tThese decisions are informed by the 14 research files in `docs/research/`:\n91\t\n92\t| Decision | Chosen Approach | Alternative Considered | Why |\n93\t|----------|----------------|----------------------|-----|\n94\t| Bead store interface | Abstract trait over `br` CLI | Direct SQLite access | Platform agnostic; works with future bead backends |\n95\t| Claim atomicity | `br update --claim` + workspace flock | Central coordinator (Perles) | No SPOF; works with decentralized workers |\n96\t| Heartbeat model | File-based heartbeat with TTL (from beads-polis) | Shared memory | Survives worker crashes; observable by peers |\n97\t| Validation gates | Pluggable gate system (inspired by bg-gate) | Hardcoded checks | Different workspaces need different validation |\n98\t| Work decomposition | Built-in mitosis with child-aware dedup | External only (spec2beads) | Mitosis is valid when the split criteria are semantic (multi-task detection) and dedup checks the parent's existing children |\n99\t| Self-modification | Allowed with release channel promotion (testing → stable → fleet hot-reload) | Prohibited entirely | v1 failures came from untested changes deploying directly to the fleet. Canary testing with defined inputs/outputs prevents this. |\n100\t| Workspace discovery | Explicit configuration | Filesystem scanning | Explore strand's unbounded find caused 35+ load |\n101\t| Alert system | Verify-then-alert with rate limiting | Alert-on-empty | 100% false positive rate from naive alerting |\n102\t\n103\t## Key Decisions from Operational Learnings\n104\t\n105\tThese decisions are informed by the 9 notes files in `docs/notes/`:\n106\t\n107\t| Learning | Design Response |\n108\t|----------|----------------|\n109\t| Mitosis explosion (5,741 duplicate beads) | Mitosis checks parent's existing children before creating new ones. Duplicate splits are structurally impossible. Split criteria are semantic (multi-task detection), not numeric. |\n110\t| 100% false positive starvation alerts | Three-state model: no beads exist / all claimed / invisible. Verify independently before alerting. |\n111\t| Bundler shipped undefined functions | Compiled language eliminates this class entirely. |\n112\t| Agent-owned closure most reliable | NEEDLE does not close beads. Agent receives `br close <id>` instruction in prompt. |\n113\t| stdout/stderr corruption | Telemetry is a structured system, never interleaved with agent output. |\n114\t| Workers modifying their own orchestrator | Self-modification allowed via release channels. New builds must pass canary tests in isolation before promotion to `:stable`. Fleet hot-reloads from `:stable`, never from `:testing`. |\n115\t| ~20 worker practical limit (EX44) | Fleet sizing is bounded by three runtime factors: provider inference throughput, available CPU, and available RAM. NEEDLE monitors these and warns when saturated. Staggered launch is default. |\n116\t| Bead granularity affects success rate | Document guidelines but don't enforce — this is a bead authoring concern, not orchestration. |\n117\t\n118\t---\n119\t\n120\t# State Machine\n121\t\n122\tThe worker loop is a finite state machine. Every state has defined entry conditions, actions, and exit transitions. There are no implicit states or fallthrough paths.\n123\t\n124\t## Worker States\n125\t\n126\t```\n127\t                    ┌──────────┐\n128\t                    │  BOOTING │\n129\t └──────────┘\n167\t```\n168\t\n169\t### Terminal States\n170\t\n171\t```\n172\t    ┌───────────┐       ┌───────────┐       ┌───────────┐\n173\t    │ EXHAUSTED │       │  STOPPED  │       │  ERRORED  │\n174\t    └───────────┘       └───────────┘       └───────────┘\n175\t    all strands empty   graceful shutdown   unrecoverable\n176\t```\n177\t\n178\t## State Definitions\n179\t\n180\t### BOOTING\n181\t\n182\t**Entry:** Worker process started.\n183\t\n184\t**Actions:**\n185\t1. Load configuration (global → workspace → CLI overrides)\n186\t2. Validate bead store connectivity (`br doctor` or equivalent)\n187\t3. Register in worker state registry\n188\t4. Emit `worker.started` telemetry event\n189\t5. Start heartbeat emitter\n190\t\n191\t**Transitions:**\n192\t| Condition | Next State |\n193\t|-----------|-----------|\n194\t| Config loaded, bead store healthy | SELECTING |\n195\t| Config invalid | ERRORED |\n196\t| Bead store unreachable | ERRORED (after retry with backoff) |\n197\t\n198\t### SELECTING\n199\t\n200\t**Entry:** Worker is ready for next bead. Every design decision in this plan traces back to one or more of them.\n\n1. **Deterministic order.** Given the same queue state, every worker computes the same bead ordering. There is no randomness in selection. Ties are broken by creation time.\n\n2. **Explicit outcome paths.** Every possible result of every operation has a named handler. If an outcome can happen, it has a handler. If it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen. The type system enforces exhaustiveness.\n\n3. **Platform and model agnostic.** NEEDLE wraps any headless CLI that accepts a prompt and exits. It runs on any POSIX system. It does not depend on any specific AI provider, model, or API.\n\n4. **Observable by default.** Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits structured telemetry. A silent worker is a broken worker. Telemetry is structured from origin (JSONL) and exportable as OpenTelemetry (OTLP) so any compliant backend — Tempo, Jaeger, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog, FABRIC — can consume NEEDLE's signals without a custom adapter.\n\n5. **Self-healing.** Workers detect and recover from stuck states, stale claims, crashed peers, and corrupted databases without human intervention. Recovery paths are explicit, not heuristic.\n\n6. **Separation of concerns.** The orchestrator does not execute work. The agent does not manage state. The bead store does not enforce workflow. Each component has one job.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nNEEDLE is composed of five layers:\n\n```\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                        CLI Layer                              │\n│  needle run | stop | list | attach | status | config          │\n├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                     Worker Layer                              │\n│  Worker loop, strand waterfall, session management            │\n├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                  Coordination Layer                            │\n│  Claiming, locking, heartbeats, peer awareness                │\n├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                    Agent Layer                                 │\n│  Adapter interface, dispatch, result capture                  │\n├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                   Foundation Layer                             │\n│  Telemetry, configuration, bead store interface, self-healing │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Component Map\n\n| Component | Responsibility | Inputs | Outputs |\n|-----------|---------------|--------|---------|\n| **CLI** | Parse commands, manage sessions | User commands | Worker processes |\n| **Worker** | Execute the strand waterfall loop | Bead queue state | Dispatch requests, state transitions |\n| **StrandRunner** | Evaluate strands in sequence | Queue state, config | Next bead or escalation |\n| **Claimer** | Atomic bead claiming with serialization | Candidate bead ID | Claimed bead or race-lost signal |\n| **PromptBuilder** | Construct deterministic prompts from bead context | Claimed bead | Prompt string |\n| **Dispatcher** | Load adapter, render template, execute agent | Prompt, adapter config | Agent process handle |\n| **OutcomeHandler** | Route exit code to explicit handler | Exit code, stdout/stderr | State transition |\n| **Telemetry** | Structured event emission | Any component event | JSONL records |\n| **HealthMonitor** | Heartbeat, stuck detection, peer awareness | Worker state | Recovery actions |\n| **ConfigLoader** | Hierarchical config resolution | Files, env, CLI args | Resolved config |\n| **BeadStore** | Abstract interface to bead backend | CRUD operations | Bead records |\n| **Mitosis** | Split multi-task beads into children with dedup | Failed bead, parent's existing children | Child beads or no-op |\n\n---\n\n## Language Decision\n\nThe implementation language must provide:\n\n| Requirement | Why | Source |\n|-------------|-----|--------|\n| Exhaustive pattern matching | Every outcome must be handled; compiler enforces it | Principle 2, bead-lifecycle-bugs.md |\n| Real module system | 45K single-file bash was unmaintainable | bash-at-scale-problems.md |\n| Structured error types | Silent failures caused cascading bugs | claim-race-conditions.md |\n| Native JSON support | Fragile jq parsing corrupted state | worker-starvation-lessons.md |\n| Proper concurrency primitives | flock/trap/PID files were inadequate | concurrency-approaches-compared.md |\n| Single binary distribution | NEEDLE must be trivially installable | Principle 3 |\n| Cross-platform | Linux, macOS at minimum | Principle 3 |\n| Static analysis | Catch undefined functions, unused variables at compile time | bundler-build-integrity.md |\n\n**Recommended: Rust.** Exhaustive `match`, `Result<T, E>` error handling, `serde_json`, `tokio` for async, single binary via static linking, cross-compilation. The beads ecosystem already has Rust precedent (beads_rust, beads-polis).\n\n**Acceptable alternative: Go.** Simpler learning curve, good concurrency, single binary. Lacks exhaustive matching (requires discipline instead of compiler enforcement).\n\n**Not acceptable: Bash, Python, Node.** Bash failed at scale (documented). Python/Node require runtime dependencies, violating single-binary distribution.\n\n---\n\n## Key Decisions from Research\n\nThese decisions are informed by the 14 research files in `docs/research/`:\n\n| Decision | Chosen Approach | Alternative Considered | Why |\n|----------|----------------|----------------------|-----|\n| Bead store interface | Abstract trait over `br` CLI | Direct SQLite access | Platform agnostic; works with future bead backends |\n| Claim atomicity | `br update --claim` + workspace flock | Central coordinator (Perles) | No SPOF; works with decentralized workers |\n| Heartbeat model | File-based heartbeat with TTL (from beads-polis) | Shared memory | Survives worker crashes; observable by peers |\n| Validation gates | Pluggable gate system (inspired by bg-gate) | Hardcoded checks | Different workspaces need different validation |\n| Work decomposition | Built-in mitosis with child-aware dedup | External only (spec2beads) | Mitosis is valid when the split criteria are semantic (multi-task detection) and dedup checks the parent's existing children |\n| Self-modification | Allowed with release channel promotion (testing → stable → fleet hot-reload) | Prohibited entirely | v1 failures came from untested changes deploying directly to the fleet. Canary testing with defined inputs/outputs prevents this. |\n| Workspace discovery | Explicit configuration | Filesystem scanning | Explore strand's unbounded find caused 35+ load |\n| Alert system | Verify-then-alert with rate limiting | Alert-on-empty | 100% false positive rate from naive alerting |\n\n## Key Decisions from Operational Learnings\n\nThese decisions are informed by the 9 notes files in `docs/notes/`:\n\n| Learning | Design Response |\n|----------|----------------|\n| Mitosis explosion (5,741 duplicate beads) | Mitosis checks parent's existing children before creating new ones. Duplicate splits are structurally impossible. Split criteria are semantic (multi-task detection), not numeric. |\n| 100% false positive starvation alerts | Three-state model: no beads exist / all claimed / invisible. Verify independently before alerting. |\n| Bundler shipped undefined functions | Compiled language eliminates this class entirely. |\n| Agent-owned closure most reliable | NEEDLE does not close beads. Agent receives `br close <id>` instruction in prompt. |\n| stdout/stderr corruption | Telemetry is a structured system, never interleaved with agent output. |\n| Workers modifying their own orchestrator | Self-modification allowed via release channels. New builds must pass canary tests in isolation before promotion to `:stable`. Fleet hot-reloads from `:stable`, never from `:testing`. |\n| ~20 worker practical limit (EX44) | Fleet sizing is bounded by three runtime factors: provider inference throughput, available CPU, and available RAM. NEEDLE monitors these and warns when saturated. Staggered launch is default. |\n| Bead granularity affects success rate | Document guidelines but don't enforce — this is a bead authoring concern, not orchestration. |\n\n---\n\n# State Machine\n\nThe worker loop is a finite state machine. Every state has defined entry conditions, actions, and exit transitions. There are no implicit states or fallthrough paths.\n\n## Worker States\n\n```\n                    ┌──────────┐\n                    │  BOOTING │\n                    └────┬─────┘\n                         │ config loaded, health check passed\n                         ▼\n                    ┌──────────┐\n              ┌────►│ SELECTING│◄──────────────────────────────┐\n              │     └────┬─────┘                                │\n              │          │ candidate found                      │\n              │          ▼                                      │\n              │     ┌──────────┐  race lost (retry < max)      │\n              │     │ CLAIMING │──────────────────────────┐    │\n              │     └────┬─────┘                           │    │\n              │          │ claimed                         │    │\n              │          ▼                                 ▼    │\n              │     ┌──────────┐                     ┌────────┐│\n              │     │ BUILDING │                     │RETRYING││\n              │     └────┬─────┘                     └────┬───┘│\n              │          │ prompt ready                   │     │\n              │          ▼                                └─────┘\n              │     ┌────────────┐\n              │     │DISPATCHING │\n              │     └────┬───────┘\n              │          │ agent process started\n              │          ▼\n              │     ┌──────────┐\n              │     │ EXECUTING│\n              │     └────┬─────┘\n              │          │ agent exited\n              │          ▼\n              │     ┌──────────┐\n              │     │ HANDLING │\n              │     └────┬─────┘\n              │          │ outcome processed\n              │          ▼\n              │     ┌──────────┐\n              │     │ LOGGING  │\n              │     └────┬─────┘\n              │          │ telemetry emitted\n              └──────────┘\n```\n\n### Terminal States\n\n```\n    ┌───────────┐       ┌───────────┐       ┌───────────┐\n    │ EXHAUSTED │       │  STOPPED  │       │  ERRORED  │\n    └───────────┘       └───────────┘       └───────────┘\n    all strands empty   graceful shutdown   unrecoverable\n```\n\n## State Definitions\n\n### BOOTING\n\n**Entry:** Worker process started.\n\n**Actions:**\n1. Load configuration (global → workspace → CLI overrides)\n2. Validate bead store connectivity (`br doctor` or equivalent)\n3. Register in worker state registry\n4. Emit `worker.started` telemetry event\n5. Start heartbeat emitter\n\n**Transitions:**\n| Condition | Next State |\n|-----------|-----------|\n| Config loaded, bead store healthy | SELECTING |\n| Config invalid | ERRORED |\n| Bead store unreachable | ERRORED (after retry with backoff) |\n\n### SELECTING\n\n**Entry:** Worker is ready for next bead. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_5515efed6c944e5fb2c727ec","type":"tool_result","content":"10-\n11-2. **Explicit outcome paths.** Every possible result of every operation has a named handler. If an outcome can happen, it has a handler. If it doesn't have a handler, it cannot happen. The type system enforces exhaustiveness.\n12-\n13-3. **Platform and model agnostic.** NEEDLE wraps any headless CLI that accepts a prompt and exits. It runs on any POSIX system. It does not depend on any specific AI provider, model, or API.\n14-\n15:4. **Observable by default.** Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits structured telemetry. A silent worker is a broken worker. Telemetry is structured from origin (JSONL) and exportable as OpenTelemetry (OTLP) so any compliant backend — Tempo, Jaeger, Grafana, Honeycomb, Datadog, FABRIC — can consume NEEDLE's signals without a custom adapter.\n16-\n17-5. **Self-healing.** Workers detect and recover from stuck states, stale claims, crashed peers, and corrupted databases without human intervention. Recovery paths are explicit, not heuristic.\n18-\n19-6. **Separation of concerns.** The orchestrator does not execute work. The agent does not manage state. The bead store does not enforce workflow. Each component has one job.\n20-\n--\n694-    event_type: String,       // e.g., \"bead.claim.attempted\"\n695-    bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n696-    workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n697-    data: serde_json::Value,  // event-specific payload\n698-    duration_ms: Option<u64>,\n699:    trace_id: Option<TraceId>,   // W3C trace ID of enclosing span, if OTLP sink enabled\n700:    span_id: Option<SpanId>,     // W3C span ID of enclosing span, if OTLP sink enabled\n701-}\n702-\n703-trait Sink: Send + Sync {\n704-    fn accept(&self, event: &TelemetryEvent) -> Result<()>;\n705-    fn flush(&self, deadline: Duration) -> Result<()>;\n706-}\n707-\n708:// Built-in sinks: FileSink, StdoutSink, HookSink, OtlpSink.\n709:// OtlpSink wraps the OpenTelemetry SDK (traces + metrics + logs providers)\n710-// and translates TelemetryEvent into the appropriate signal per the\n711-// Semantic Mapping table in the Telemetry chapter.\n712-```\n713-\n714-### health\n--\n1560-\n1561-- Events matching the filter are piped as JSON to the command's stdin\n1562-- Hook execution is fire-and-forget (non-blocking)\n1563-- Failed hooks emit a `telemetry.hook.failed` event to the file sink (not recursively to hooks)\n1564-\n1565:### OTLP Sink (optional)\n1566-\n1567:Exports telemetry as OpenTelemetry signals (traces, metrics, logs) over OTLP to any compliant collector (OpenTelemetry Collector, Jaeger, Tempo, Grafana Alloy, Honeycomb, Datadog, etc.). This is the canonical integration point for FABRIC and any downstream observability plane.\n1568-\n1569-```yaml\n1570-telemetry:\n1571:  otlp:\n1572-    enabled: true\n1573-    endpoint: \"http://otel-collector.tailnet:4317\"    # gRPC default; use 4318 for HTTP\n1574-    protocol: grpc                                     # grpc | http/protobuf\n1575-    headers:\n1576-      authorization: \"Bearer ${OTEL_TOKEN}\"            # env interpolation\n--\n1588-      service.namespace: \"needle-fleet\"\n1589-```\n1590-\n1591-Design:\n1592-\n1593:- **Non-blocking.** Uses a batch span/log/metric processor. If the collector is unreachable, events are buffered up to a bounded queue, then dropped (same policy as file sink). Drops emit a `telemetry.otlp.dropped` event to the file sink (never recursively to OTLP).\n1594:- **Additive.** The file sink is authoritative. OTLP is an export, not a replacement. If OTLP is disabled or misconfigured, NEEDLE behaves identically to a file-sink-only deployment.\n1595:- **Stdlib deps.** Rust crates: `opentelemetry`, `opentelemetry_sdk`, `opentelemetry-otlp`, `opentelemetry-semantic-conventions`. All support OTLP/gRPC and OTLP/HTTP.\n1596-- **W3C trace context.** Trace and span IDs are generated per the W3C Trace Context spec so they interop with any OTel backend.\n1597-- **Graceful shutdown.** On `worker.stopped`, the exporter is flushed with a deadline before process exit. Failure to flush is logged but never blocks shutdown.\n1598-\n1599-## OpenTelemetry Semantic Mapping\n1600-\n1601:NEEDLE's existing event catalog maps cleanly to OpenTelemetry's three signal types. This mapping is **normative** — the OTLP sink implementation must conform to it so dashboards and alerts remain stable across NEEDLE versions.\n1602-\n1603-### Resource Attributes\n1604-\n1605-Every exported signal carries these resource attributes (per OTel semantic conventions):\n1606-\n--\n2151-  stdout_sink:\n2152-    enabled: false\n2153-    format: normal\n2154-    color: auto\n2155-  hooks: []\n2156:  otlp:\n2157-    enabled: false\n2158-    endpoint: \"http://localhost:4317\"\n2159-    protocol: grpc              # grpc | http/protobuf\n2160-    headers: {}                 # e.g., authorization: \"Bearer ${OTEL_TOKEN}\"\n2161-    timeout_ms: 5000\n--\n2683-- Peer monitoring\n2684-- Workspace config (.needle.yaml)\n2685-- Multiple agent adapters\n2686-- Cost tracking\n2687-- Budget enforcement\n2688:- OTLP sink (Phase 2; Phase 1 ships JSONL file sink only)\n2689-- `needle attach`, `needle status`, `needle config`\n2690-\n2691-### Success Criteria\n2692-\n2693-- [ ] `needle run --workspace /path --agent claude-anthropic-sonnet` launches a worker in tmux\n--\n2725-| **Cost tracking** | Token extraction, pricing config, effort events |\n2726-| **Budget enforcement** | Warn/stop at daily cost thresholds |\n2727-| **CLI extensions** | `needle attach`, `needle status`, `needle config` |\n2728-| **Database recovery** | Auto-detect corruption, repair from JSONL |\n2729-| **Mitosis** | Child-aware bead splitting on first failure, with dedup and flock serialization |\n2730:| **OTLP sink** | OpenTelemetry exporter emitting traces, metrics, and logs per the semantic mapping in the Telemetry chapter. gRPC + HTTP/protobuf transports. It does not depend on any specific AI provider, model, or API.\n14-\n15:4. **Observable by default.** Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits structured telemetry. Structured telemetry — JSONL event stream, never on stdout/stderr.\n//!\n//! Every state transition, claim attempt, dispatch, and outcome emits a typed\n//! event. The emitter is non-blocking: events are queued and written by a\n//! background task. A broken sink never blocks or panics the worker.\n//!\n//! ## Architecture\n//! ```text\n//! worker → emit() → mpsc::Sender → [background task] → TelemetryBus → Vec<Box<dyn Sink>>\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse tokio::sync::mpsc;\n\nuse crate::config::{ColorMode, HookConfig, StdoutFormat, StdoutSinkConfig, TelemetryConfig};\nuse crate::types::{BeadId, WorkerId, WorkerState};\n\n// ─── TelemetryEvent ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single structured telemetry record.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryEvent {\n    /// UTC timestamp with millisecond precision.\n    pub timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Discriminant string, e.g. `\"state_transition\"`.\n    pub event_type: String,\n    /// The worker that emitted this event.\n    pub worker_id: WorkerId,\n    /// Session identifier (8 hex chars, unique per worker boot).\n    pub session_id: String,\n    /// Monotonically increasing counter within the session.\n    pub sequence: u64,\n    /// Bead context when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub bead_id: Option<BeadId>,\n    /// Workspace directory when relevant.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    /// Event-specific payload as a JSON value.\n    pub data: serde_json::Value,\n    /// Optional duration in milliseconds for timed operations.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,\n    /// W3C trace-id hex (32 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub trace_id: Option<String>,\n    /// W3C span-id hex (16 lowercase hex chars). Present only when emitted inside an OTel span.\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub span_id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n// ─── EventKind ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Typed event variants emitted by all NEEDLE components.\n///\n/// Every variant maps to a `TelemetryEvent` with `event_type` matching\n/// the snake_case discriminant name.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n#[non_exhaustive]\npub enum EventKind {\n    // ── Worker lifecycle ──\n    WorkerBooting {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStarted {\n        worker_name: String,\n        version: String,\n    },\n    WorkerStopped {\n        reason: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n        uptime_secs: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerErrored {\n        error_type: String,\n        error_message: String,\n        beads_processed: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerExhausted {\n        cycle_count: u64,\n        last_strand: String,\n        /// How many times the waterfall restarted before exhausting.\n        waterfall_restarts: u32,\n        /// Name of each strand that returned WorkCreated (one entry per restart).\n        restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n        /// All strand evaluations in order, across all waterfall passes.\n        /// Each entry is (strand_name, result, duration_ms) for diagnostic visibility.\n        strand_evaluations: Vec<(String, String, u64)>,\n    },\n    WorkerIdle {\n        backoff_seconds: u64,\n    },\n    StateTransition {\n        from: WorkerState,\n        to: WorkerState,\n    },\n    InitStepStarted {\n        step: String,\n    },\n    InitStepCompleted {\n        step: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    WorkerBootTimeout {\n        elapsed_ms: u64,\n    },\n\n    // ── Strand evaluation ──\n    StrandEvaluated {\n        strand_name: String,\n        result: String,\n        duration_ms: u64,\n    },\n    StrandSkipped {\n        strand_name: String,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    QueueEmpty,\n\n    // ── Bead claim ──\n    ClaimAttempt {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        attempt: u32,\n    },\n    ClaimSuccess {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLost {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n    },\n    ClaimFailed {\n        bead_id: BeadId,\n        reason: String,\n    },\n    ClaimRaceLostSkipped {\n        consecutive_losses: u32,\n        threshold: u32,\n    },\n\n    // ── Bead lifecycle ──\n    BeadReleased {","numLines":150,"startLine":1,"totalLines":4803}}}

Returns `Ok(None)` if OTLP is disabled in config.\n105\t    pub fn new(config: &OtlpConfig, worker_attrs: &WorkerAttrs) -> Result<Option<Self>> {\n106\t        if !config.enabled {\n107\t .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n350\t            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n351\t\n352\t        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip   if config.tls.insecure {\n550\t            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n551\t                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n552\t                    .with_insecure(true)\n553\t            );\n554\t        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n555\t            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n556\t                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n557\t            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n558\t                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n559\t                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n560\t            );\n561\t        }\n562\t\n563\t        let mut client =    }\n575\t\n576\t    /// Create null metric instruments (used when metrics are disabled).\n577\t    fn null_metrics() -> Metrics {\n578\t        use opentelemetry::metrics::MeterProvider as _;\n579\t        use opentelemetry_sdk::metrics::SdkMeterProvider;\n580\t\n581\t        // Create a no-op meter provider\n582\t        let provider = SdkMeterProvider::builder().build();\n583\t        let meter .with_body(body.into())\n642\t            .with_attributes(attrs)\n643\t            .build();\n644\t\n645\t        let _ = logger.log(log_record);\n646\t    }\n647\t\n648\t    /// Map event type to         | \"telemetry.hook.failed\"\n658\t            | \"telemetry.otlp.dropped\"\n659\t            | \"telemetry.file.failed\"\n660\t            => sdk_logs::Severity::Error,\n661\t\n662\t            // WARN events\n663\t            | \"peer.stale\"\n664\t            | \"bead.orphaned\"\n665\t            | \"stuck.detected\"\n666\t            | \"claim.race_lost.skipped\"\n667\t            | sdk_logs::Severity::Warn,\n672\t\n673\t            // Everything else is INFO\n674\t            _ => sdk_logs::Severity::Info,\n675\t        }\n676\t    }\n677\t}\n678\t\n679\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n680\t// Sink trait implementation\n681\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n682\t\n683\timpl crate::telemetry::Sink for OtlpSink {\n684\t    /// Accept a telemetry event and dispatch it to the appropriate signal(s).\n685\t    fn accept(&self, event: &TelemetryEvent) -> Result<()> {\n686\t        // Metrics are emitted for specific event types\n687\t        if self.metrics_enabled {\n688\t Result<()> {\n705\t        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n706\t\n707\t        // Split deadline across providers\n708\t        let per_provider = deadline / 3;\n709\t\n710\t        if let .context(\"failed to flush tracer provider\")?;\n715\t            }\n716\t        }\n717\t\n718\t        if let Some(provider) = &self.meter_provider {\n719\t            if start.elapsed() < deadline .force_flush(per_provider)\n730\t                    .context(\"failed to flush logger provider\")?;\n731\t            }\n732\t        }\n733\t\n734\t        Ok(())\n735\t          // No metric for this event type\n855\t            }\n856\t        }\n857\t    }\n858\t\n859\t    /// Shutdown all providers gracefully.\n860\t    pub fn shutdown(&self) -> Result<()> {\n861\t        if let Some(provider) = &self.tracer_provider {\n862\t            provider.shutdown().context(\"failed to shutdown tracer provider\")?;\n863\t        }\n864\t\n865\t        if let timestamp_nanos.unsigned_abs() as u64,\n902\t        );\n903\t        if timestamp_nanos >= 0 {\n904\t            SystemTime::UNIX_EPOCH + duration\n905\t        } else {\n906\t OpenTelemetry OTLP sink — exports traces, metrics, and logs.\n//!\n//! This module is gated behind the `otlp` feature. When enabled, it provides\n//! the `OtlpSink` which implements the `Sink` trait and exports telemetry to\n//! any OTLP-compatible collector.\n\n#![cfg(feature = \"otlp\")]\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse opentelemetry::metrics::MeterProvider;\nuse opentelemetry::trace::TracerProvider;\nuse opentelemetry::{Key, KeyValue};\nuse opentelemetry_sdk::metrics::{\n    MeterProvider as SdkMeterProvider, PeriodicReader, SdkMeterProviderBuilder,\n};\nuse opentelemetry_sdk::resource::{\n    Resource, ResourceDetector, TelemetryResourceDetector,\n};\nuse opentelemetry_sdk::trace::{BatchSpanProcessor, Config, TracerProvider as SdkTracerProvider};\nuse opentelemetry_sdk::{\n    logs as sdk_logs,\n    logs::{LogBatch, LogEvent, LogResult},\n    runtime,\n};\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"otlp\")]\nuse opentelemetry_otlp::{\n    ExportConfig, WithExportConfig, WithHttpExportConfig, WithTonicExportConfig,\n    HttpExportConfig, TonicExportConfig,\n};\n#[cfg(feature = \"otlp\")]\nuse opentelemetry_semantic_conventions as semcov;\n\nuse crate::config::OtlpConfig;\nuse crate::telemetry::TelemetryEvent;\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// OtlpSink\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// OTLP sink that exports telemetry to an OpenTelemetry collector.\n///\n/// This sink creates and manages three OpenTelemetry SDK providers:\n/// - `TracerProvider` for distributed traces\n/// - `MeterProvider` for metrics\n/// - `LoggerProvider` for structured logs\n///\n/// Each provider uses batch processing to avoid blocking the worker thread.\npub struct OtlpSink {\n    /// OpenTelemetry resource attributes (service.name, host.name, etc.).\n    resource: Resource,\n    /// Tracer provider for span export.\n    tracer_provider: Option<Arc<SdkTracerProvider>>,\n    /// Meter provider for metric export.\n    meter_provider: Option<Arc<SdkMeterProvider>>,\n    /// Logger provider for log export.\n    logger_provider: Option<Arc<sdk_logs::LoggerProvider>>,\n    /// Instrument handles for metric emission (cached to avoid repeated lookups).\n    metrics: Metrics,\n    /// Whether trace export is enabled.\n    traces_enabled: bool,\n    /// Whether metric export is enabled.\n    metrics_enabled: bool,\n    /// Whether log export is enabled.\n    logs_enabled: bool,\n}\n\n/// Cached metric instrument handles.\n///\n/// These are created once at initialization and reused for all metric\n/// emissions to avoid the overhead of repeated instrument lookups.\nstruct Metrics {\n    /// `needle.workers.active` — UpDownCounter.\n    workers_active: opentelemetry::metrics::UpDownCounter<i64>,\n    /// `needle.beads.claimed` — Counter.\n    beads_claimed: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<u64>,\n    /// `needle.beads.completed` — Counter.\n    beads_completed: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<u64>,\n    /// `needle.beads.duration` — Histogram.\n    beads_duration: opentelemetry::metrics::Histogram<f64>,\n    /// `needle.claim.attempts` — Counter.\n    claim_attempts: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<u64>,\n    /// `needle.strand.duration` — Histogram.\n    strand_duration: opentelemetry::metrics::Histogram<f64>,\n    /// `needle.agent.duration` — Histogram.\n    agent_duration: opentelemetry::metrics::Histogram<f64>,\n    /// `needle.agent.tokens.input` — Counter.\n    agent_tokens_input: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<u64>,\n    /// `needle.agent.tokens.output` — Counter.\n    agent_tokens_output: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<u64>,\n    /// `needle.cost.usd` — Counter.\n    cost_usd: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<f64>,\n    /// `needle.mitosis.children_created` — Counter.\n    mitosis_children_created: opentelemetry::metrics::Counter<u64>,\n}\n\nimpl OtlpSink {\n    /// Create a new OTLP sink from the given configuration.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Ok(None)` if OTLP is disabled in config.\n    pub fn new(config: &OtlpConfig, worker_attrs: &WorkerAttrs) -> Result<Option<Self>> {\n        if !config.enabled {\n            return Ok(None);\n        }\n\n        let resource = Self::build_resource(config, worker_attrs)?;\n\n        // Build exporters based on protocol\n        let tracer_provider = if config.signals.traces {\n            Some(Self::build_tracer_provider(config, resource.clone())?)\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        let (meter_provider, metrics) = if config.signals.metrics {\n            let (provider, metrics) = Self::build_meter_provider(config, resource.clone())?;\n            (Some(provider), metrics)\n        } else {\n            (None, Self::null_metrics())\n        };\n\n        let logger_provider = if config.signals.logs {\n            Some(Self::build_logger_provider(config, resource.clone())?)\n        } else {\n            None\n        };\n\n        Ok(Some(Self {\n            resource,\n            tracer_provider,\n            meter_provider,\n            logger_provider,\n            metrics,\n            traces_enabled: config.signals.traces,\n            metrics_enabled: config.signals.metrics,\n            logs_enabled: config.signals.logs,\n        }))\n    }\n\n    /// Build the OpenTelemetry Resource from config and computed attributes.\n    fn build_resource(config: &OtlpConfig, worker_attrs: &WorkerAttrs) -> Result<Resource> {\n        let mut attrs = Vec::new();\n\n        // Service attributes (OTel semantic conventions)\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(semcov::resource::SERVICE_NAME, \"needle\"));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\n            semcov::resource::SERVICE_VERSION,\n            env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\"),\n        ));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\n            semcov::resource::SERVICE_INSTANCE_ID,\n            worker_attrs.worker_id.clone(),\n        ));\n\n        // Host attributes\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\n            semcov::resource::HOST_NAME,\n            worker_attrs.hostname.clone(),\n        ));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\n            semcov::resource::PROCESS_PID,\n            worker_attrs.pid.to_string(),\n        ));\n\n        // Needle-specific attributes\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"needle.agent\", worker_attrs.agent.clone()));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"needle.model\", worker_attrs.model.clone()));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"needle.session_id\", worker_attrs.session_id.clone()));\n        if let Some(workspace) = &worker_attrs.workspace {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"needle.workspace\", workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string()));\n        }\n\n        // User-provided resource attributes (overrides if duplicate)\n        for (k, v) in &config.resource_attributes {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(k.clone(), v.clone()));\n        }\n\n        // Apply defaults for common attributes if not provided\n        let has_namespace = attrs\n            .iter()\n            .any(|kv| kv.key.as_str() == semcov::resource::SERVICE_NAMESPACE);\n        if !has_namespace {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(semcov::resource::SERVICE_NAMESPACE, \"needle-fleet\"));\n        }\n\n        Ok(Resource::new(attrs))\n    }\n\n    /// Build the TracerProvider with batch span processor.\n    fn build_tracer_provider(config: &OtlpConfig, resource: Resource) -> Result<Arc<SdkTracerProvider>> {\n        let exporter = match config.protocol {\n            crate::config::OtlpProtocol::Grpc => {\n                Self::build_grpc_span_exporter(config)?\n            }\n            crate::config::OtlpProtocol::HttpProto => {\n                Self::build_http_span_exporter(config)?\n            }\n        };\n\n        let builder = SdkTracerProvider::builder()\n            .with_resource(resource)\n            .with_batch_exporter(exporter, runtime::Tokio);\n\n        Ok(Arc::new(builder.build()))\n    }\n\n    /// Build the MeterProvider with periodic reader.\n    fn build_meter_provider(\n        config: &OtlpConfig,\n        resource: Resource,\n    ) -> Result<(Arc<SdkMeterProvider>, Metrics)> {\n        let exporter = match config.protocol {\n            crate::config::OtlpProtocol::Grpc => {\n                Self::build_grpc_metric_exporter(config)?\n            }\n            crate::config::OtlpProtocol::HttpProto => {\n                Self::build_http_metric_exporter(config)?\n            }\n        };\n\n        let period = Duration::from_secs(10);\n        let reader = PeriodicReader::builder(exporter, runtime::Tokio)\n            .with_interval(period)\n            .build();\n\n        let provider = SdkMeterProvider::builder()\n            .with_resource(resource)\n            .with_reader(reader)\n            .build();\n\n        let meter = provider.meter(\"needle\");\n\n        // Create metric instruments\n        let workers_active = meter\n            .i64_up_down_counter(\"needle.workers.active\")\n            .with_unit(\"{worker}\")\n            .with_description(\"Current live worker count\")\n            .init();\n\n        let beads_claimed = meter\n            .u64_counter(\"needle.beads.claimed\")\n            .with_unit(\"{bead}\")\n            .with_description(\"Successful bead claims\")\n            .init();\n\n        let beads_completed = meter\n            .u64_counter(\"needle.beads.completed\")\n            .with_unit(\"{bead}\")\n            .with_description(\"Bead terminal outcomes\")\n            .init();\n\n        let beads_duration = meter\n            .f64_histogram(\"needle.beads.duration\")\n            .with_unit(\"ms\")\n            .with_description(\"End-to-end bead lifecycle time\")\n            .init();\n\n        let claim_attempts = meter\n            .u64_counter(\"needle.claim.attempts\")\n            .with_unit(\"{attempt}\")\n            .with_description(\"Claim attempts\")\n            .init();\n\n        let strand_duration = meter\n            .f64_histogram(\"needle.strand.duration\")\n            .with_unit(\"ms\")\n            .with_description(\"Strand evaluation time\")\n            .init();\n\n        let agent_duration = meter\n            .f64_histogram(\"needle.agent.duration\")\n            .with_unit(\"ms\")\n            .with_description(\"Agent process runtime\")\n            .init();\n\n        let agent_tokens_input = meter\n            .u64_counter(\"needle.agent.tokens.input\")\n            .with_unit(\"{token}\")\n            .with_description(\"Input tokens consumed\")\n            .init();\n\n        let agent_tokens_output = meter\n            .u64_counter(\"needle.agent.tokens.output\")\n            .with_unit(\"{token}\")\n            .with_description(\"Output tokens produced\")\n            .init();\n\n        let cost_usd = meter\n            .f64_counter(\"needle.cost.usd\")\n            .with_unit(\"USD\")\n            .with_description(\"Estimated cost accumulator\")\n            .init();\n\n        let mitosis_children_created = meter\n            .u64_counter(\"needle.mitosis.children_created\")\n            .with_unit(\"{bead}\")\n            .with_description(\"Mitosis child creations\")\n            .init();\n\n        let metrics = Metrics {\n            workers_active,\n            beads_claimed,\n            beads_completed,\n            beads_duration,\n            claim_attempts,\n            strand_duration,\n            agent_duration,\n            agent_tokens_input,\n            agent_tokens_output,\n            cost_usd,\n            mitosis_children_created,\n        };\n\n        Ok((Arc::new(provider), metrics))\n    }\n\n    /// Build the LoggerProvider with batch log processor.\n    fn build_logger_provider(\n        config: &OtlpConfig,\n        resource: Resource,\n    ) -> Result<Arc<sdk_logs::LoggerProvider>> {\n        let exporter = match config.protocol {\n            crate::config::OtlpProtocol::Grpc => {\n                Self::build_grpc_log_exporter(config)?\n            }\n            crate::config::OtlpProtocol::HttpProto => {\n                Self::build_http_log_exporter(config)?\n            }\n        };\n\n        let processor = sdk_logs::BatchLogProcessor::builder(exporter, runtime::Tokio).build();\n\n        let provider = sdk_logs::LoggerProvider::builder()\n            .with_resource(resource)\n            .with_log_processor(processor)\n            .build();\n\n        Ok(Arc::new(provider))\n    }\n\n    /// Build a gRPC span exporter.\n    #[allow(clippy::unused_async)]\n    fn build_grpc_span_exporter(config: &OtlpConfig) -> Result<opentelemetry_otlp::SpanExporter> {\n        let mut export_config = TonicExportConfig::default()\n            .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n\n        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip {\n            export_config = export_config.with_compression(opentelemetry_otlp::Compression::Gzip);\n        }\n\n        if config.tls.insecure {\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_insecure(true)\n            );\n        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n            // Load custom CA certificate\n            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n            );\n        }\n\n        let mut client = opentelemetry_otlp::new_exporter()\n            .tonic()\n            .with_export_config(export_config);\n\n        // Add headers\n        for (k, v) in &config.headers {\n            client = client.with_metadata(k.clone(), v.clone());\n        }\n\n        client\n            .build_span_exporter()\n            .context(\"failed to build gRPC span exporter\")\n    }\n\n    /// Build an HTTP/protobuf span exporter.\n    #[allow(clippy::unused_async)]\n    fn build_http_span_exporter(config: &OtlpConfig) -> Result<opentelemetry_otlp::SpanExporter> {\n        let mut export_config = HttpExportConfig::default()\n            .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n\n        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip {\n            export_config = export_config.with_compression(opentelemetry_otlp::Compression::Gzip);\n        }\n\n        if config.tls.insecure {\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_insecure(true)\n            );\n        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n            );\n        }\n\n        let mut client = opentelemetry_otlp::new_exporter()\n            .http()\n            .with_export_config(export_config);\n\n        // Add headers\n        for (k, v) in &config.headers {\n            client = client.with_metadata(k.clone(), v.clone());\n        }\n\n        client\n            .build_span_exporter()\n            .context(\"failed to build HTTP span exporter\")\n    }\n\n    /// Build a gRPC metric exporter.\n    #[allow(clippy::unused_async)]\n    fn build_grpc_metric_exporter(config: &OtlpConfig) -> Result<opentelemetry_otlp::MetricExporter> {\n        let mut export_config = TonicExportConfig::default()\n            .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n\n        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip {\n            export_config = export_config.with_compression(opentelemetry_otlp::Compression::Gzip);\n        }\n\n        if config.tls.insecure {\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_insecure(true)\n            );\n        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n            );\n        }\n\n        let mut client = opentelemetry_otlp::new_exporter()\n            .tonic()\n            .with_export_config(export_config);\n\n        for (k, v) in &config.headers {\n            client = client.with_metadata(k.clone(), v.clone());\n        }\n\n        client\n            .build_metrics_exporter()\n            .context(\"failed to build gRPC metric exporter\")\n    }\n\n    /// Build an HTTP/protobuf metric exporter.\n    #[allow(clippy::unused_async)]\n    fn build_http_metric_exporter(config: &OtlpConfig) -> Result<opentelemetry_otlp::MetricExporter> {\n        let mut export_config = HttpExportConfig::default()\n            .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n\n        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip {\n            export_config = export_config.with_compression(opentelemetry_otlp::Compression::Gzip);\n        }\n\n        if config.tls.insecure {\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_insecure(true)\n            );\n        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n            );\n        }\n\n        let mut client = opentelemetry_otlp::new_exporter()\n            .http()\n            .with_export_config(export_config);\n\n        for (k, v) in &config.headers {\n            client = client.with_metadata(k.clone(), v.clone());\n        }\n\n        client\n            .build_metrics_exporter()\n            .context(\"failed to build HTTP metric exporter\")\n    }\n\n    /// Build a gRPC log exporter.\n    #[allow(clippy::unused_async)]\n    fn build_grpc_log_exporter(config: &OtlpConfig) -> Result<opentelemetry_otlp::LogExporter> {\n        let mut export_config = TonicExportConfig::default()\n            .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n\n        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip {\n            export_config = export_config.with_compression(opentelemetry_otlp::Compression::Gzip);\n        }\n\n        if config.tls.insecure {\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_insecure(true)\n            );\n        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n            );\n        }\n\n        let mut client = opentelemetry_otlp::new_exporter()\n            .tonic()\n            .with_export_config(export_config);\n\n        for (k, v) in &config.headers {\n            client = client.with_metadata(k.clone(), v.clone());\n        }\n\n        client\n            .build_log_exporter()\n            .context(\"failed to build gRPC log exporter\")\n    }\n\n    /// Build an HTTP/protobuf log exporter.\n    #[allow(clippy::unused_async)]\n    fn build_http_log_exporter(config: &OtlpConfig) -> Result<opentelemetry_otlp::LogExporter> {\n        let mut export_config = HttpExportConfig::default()\n            .with_endpoint(config.endpoint.clone())\n            .with_timeout(Duration::from_millis(config.timeout_ms));\n\n        if config.compression == crate::config::OtlpCompression::Gzip {\n            export_config = export_config.with_compression(opentelemetry_otlp::Compression::Gzip);\n        }\n\n        if config.tls.insecure {\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_insecure(true)\n            );\n        } else if !config.tls.ca_file.is_empty() {\n            let ca_file = std::fs::read_to_string(&config.tls.ca_file)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read CA file: {}\", config.tls.ca_file))?;\n            export_config = export_config.with_tls_config(\n                opentelemetry_otlp::TlsConfig::new()\n                    .with_ca_cert(ca_file)\n            );\n        }\n\n        let mut client = opentelemetry_otlp::new_exporter()\n            .http()\n            .with_export_config(export_config);\n\n        for (k, v) in &config.headers {\n            client = client.with_metadata(k.clone(), v.clone());\n        }\n\n        client\n            .build_log_exporter()\n            .context(\"failed to build HTTP log exporter\")\n    }\n\n    /// Create null metric instruments (used when metrics are disabled).\n    fn null_metrics() -> Metrics {\n        use opentelemetry::metrics::MeterProvider as _;\n        use opentelemetry_sdk::metrics::SdkMeterProvider;\n\n        // Create a no-op meter provider\n        let provider = SdkMeterProvider::builder().build();\n        let meter = provider.meter(\"null\");\n\n        Metrics {\n            workers_active: meter.i64_up_down_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            beads_claimed: meter.u64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            beads_completed: meter.u64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            beads_duration: meter.f64_histogram(\"null\").build(),\n            claim_attempts: meter.u64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            strand_duration: meter.f64_histogram(\"null\").build(),\n            agent_duration: meter.f64_histogram(\"null\").build(),\n            agent_tokens_input: meter.u64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            agent_tokens_output: meter.u64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            cost_usd: meter.f64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n            mitosis_children_created: meter.u64_counter(\"null\").build(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Emit a log record if logs are enabled.\n    fn emit_log(&self, event: &TelemetryEvent) {\n        if !self.logs_enabled {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let Some(provider) = &self.logger_provider else {\n            return;\n        };\n\n        let logger = provider.logger(\"needle\");\n\n        // Determine severity\n        let severity = self.severity_for_event(&event.event_type);\n\n        // Build attributes from event\n        let mut attrs = Vec::new();\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"event_type\", event.event_type.clone()));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"worker_id\", event.worker_id.clone()));\n        attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"session_id\", event.session_id.clone()));\n        if let Some(bead_id) = &event.bead_id {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"bead_id\", bead_id.to_string()));\n        }\n        if let Some(workspace) = &event.workspace {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"workspace\", workspace.to_string_lossy().to_string()));\n        }\n        if let Some(trace_id) = &event.trace_id {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"trace_id\", trace_id.clone()));\n        }\n        if let Some(span_id) = &event.span_id {\n            attrs.push(KeyValue::new(\"span_id\", span_id.clone()));\n        }\n\n        // Convert timestamp\n        let timestamp = event.timestamp.into();\n        let body = serde_json::to_string(&event.data).unwrap_or_else(|_| \"{}\".to_string());\n\n        let log_record = sdk_logs::LogRecord::builder()\n            .with_timestamp(timestamp)\n            .with_observed_timestamp(SystemTime::now())\n            .with_severity(severity)\n            .with_body(body.into())\n            .with_attributes(attrs)\n            .build();\n\n        let _ = logger.log(log_record);\n    }\n\n    /// Map event type to log severity.\n    fn severity_for_event(&self, event_type: &str) -> sdk_logs::Severity {\n        match event_type {\n            // ERROR events\n            | \"worker.errored\"\n            | \"bead.claim.failed\"\n            | \"bead.release.failed\"\n            | \"build.timeout\"\n            | \"worker.handling.timeout\"\n            | \"telemetry.hook.failed\"\n            | \"telemetry.otlp.dropped\"\n            | \"telemetry.file.failed\"\n            => sdk_logs::Severity::Error,\n\n            // WARN events\n            | \"peer.stale\"\n            | \"bead.orphaned\"\n            | \"stuck.detected\"\n            | \"claim.race_lost.skipped\"\n            | \"strand.skipped\"\n            | \"mend.dependency_cleanup_failed\"\n            | \"mend.bead_release_failed\"\n            | \"mend.lock_remove_failed\"\n            => sdk_logs::Severity::Warn,\n\n            // Everything else is INFO\n            _ => sdk_logs::Severity::Info,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Sink trait implementation\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimpl crate::telemetry::Sink for OtlpSink {\n    /// Accept a telemetry event and dispatch it to the appropriate signal(s).\n    fn accept(&self, event: &TelemetryEvent) -> Result<()> {\n        // Metrics are emitted for specific event types\n        if self.metrics_enabled {\n            self.emit_metrics(event);\n        }\n\n        // Logs are emitted for all events (except those that are already spans)\n        if self.logs_enabled {\n            self.emit_log(event);\n        }\n\n        // Note: Spans are created in the worker code, not here.\n        // The OtlpSink only handles metrics and logs.\n        // Spans are managed via the tracing-opentelemetry layer.\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Flush all providers before shutdown.\n    fn flush(&self, deadline: Duration) -> Result<()> {\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        // Split deadline across providers\n        let per_provider = deadline / 3;\n\n        if let Some(provider) = &self.tracer_provider {\n            if start.elapsed() < deadline {\n                provider\n                    .force_flush(per_provider)\n                    .context(\"failed to flush tracer provider\")?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if let Some(provider) = &self.meter_provider {\n            if start.elapsed() < deadline {\n                provider\n                    .force_flush(per_provider)\n                    .context(\"failed to flush meter provider\")?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if let Some(provider) = &self.logger_provider {\n            if start.elapsed() < deadline {\n                provider\n                    .force_flush(per_provider)\n                    .context(\"failed to flush logger provider\")?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl OtlpSink {\n    /// Emit metrics for the given event.\n    fn emit_metrics(&self, event: &TelemetryEvent) {\n        match event.event_type.as_str() {\n            \"worker.started\" => {\n                self.metrics.workers_active.add(1, &[]);\n            }\n            \"worker.stopped\" => {\n                self.metrics.workers_active.add(-1, &[]);\n            }\n            \"bead.claim.succeeded\" => {\n                let strand = event.data.get(\"strand\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                let priority = event.data.get(\"priority\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(0);\n                self.metrics.beads_claimed.add(\n                    1,\n                    &[\n                        KeyValue::new(\"strand\", strand.to_string()),\n                        KeyValue::new(\"priority\", priority as i64),\n                    ],\n                );\n            }\n            \"bead.outcome.handled\" => {\n                let outcome = event.data.get(\"outcome\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                self.metrics.beads_completed.add(\n                    1,\n                    &[KeyValue::new(\"outcome\", outcome.to_string())],\n                );\n\n                if let Some(duration_ms) = event.duration_ms {\n                    self.metrics.beads_duration.record(\n                        duration_ms as f64,\n                        &[KeyValue::new(\"outcome\", outcome.to_string())],\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            \"bead.claim.attempted\" => {\n                let result = event.data.get(\"result\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                self.metrics.claim_attempts.add(\n                    1,\n                    &[KeyValue::new(\"result\", result.to_string())],\n                );\n            }\n            \"strand.evaluated\" => {\n                if let Some(duration_ms) = event.duration_ms {\n                    let strand_name = event.data.get(\"strand_name\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let result = event.data.get(\"result\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    self.metrics.strand_duration.record(\n                        duration_ms as f64,\n                        &[\n                            KeyValue::new(\"strand\", strand_name.to_string()),\n                            KeyValue::new(\"result\", result.to_string()),\n                        ],\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            \"dispatch.completed\" => {\n                if let Some(duration_ms) = event.duration_ms {\n                    let agent = event.data.get(\"agent\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let model = event.data.get(\"model\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let exit_code = event.data.get(\"exit_code\").and_then(|v| v.as_i64()).unwrap_or(0);\n                    self.metrics.agent_duration.record(\n                        duration_ms as f64,\n                        &[\n                            KeyValue::new(\"agent\", agent.to_string()),\n                            KeyValue::new(\"model\", model.to_string()),\n                            KeyValue::new(\"exit_code\", exit_code),\n                        ],\n                    );\n                }\n\n                // Token and cost metrics (if present)\n                if let Some(input_tokens) = event.data.get(\"input_tokens\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                    let agent = event.data.get(\"agent\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let model = event.data.get(\"model\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    self.metrics.agent_tokens_input.add(\n                        input_tokens,\n                        &[\n                            KeyValue::new(\"agent\", agent.to_string()),\n                            KeyValue::new(\"model\", model.to_string()),\n                        ],\n                    );\n                }\n\n                if let Some(output_tokens) = event.data.get(\"output_tokens\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                    let agent = event.data.get(\"agent\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let model = event.data.get(\"model\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    self.metrics.agent_tokens_output.add(\n                        output_tokens,\n                        &[\n                            KeyValue::new(\"agent\", agent.to_string()),\n                            KeyValue::new(\"model\", model.to_string()),\n                        ],\n                    );\n                }\n\n                if let Some(cost_usd) = event.data.get(\"cost_usd\").and_then(|v| v.as_f64()) {\n                    let agent = event.data.get(\"agent\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    let model = event.data.get(\"model\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(\"unknown\");\n                    self.metrics.cost_usd.add(\n                        cost_usd,\n                        &[\n                            KeyValue::new(\"agent\", agent.to_string()),\n                            KeyValue::new(\"model\", model.to_string()),\n                        ],\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            \"mitosis.child_created\" => {\n                if let Some(parent_id) = event.bead_id.as_ref() {\n                    self.metrics.mitosis_children_created.add(\n                        1,\n                        &[KeyValue::new(\"parent_id\", parent_id.to_string())],\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n            _ => {\n                // No metric for this event type\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Shutdown all providers gracefully.\n    pub fn shutdown(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        if let Some(provider) = &self.tracer_provider {\n            provider.shutdown().context(\"failed to shutdown tracer provider\")?;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(provider) = &self.meter_provider {\n            provider.shutdown().context(\"failed to shutdown meter provider\")?;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(provider) = &self.logger_provider {\n            provider.shutdown().context(\"failed to shutdown logger provider\")?;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Worker attributes for resource construction\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Worker-specific attributes used to build the OpenTelemetry resource.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WorkerAttrs {\n    pub worker_id: String,\n    pub agent: String,\n    pub model: String,\n    pub session_id: String,\n    pub pid: u32,\n    pub hostname: String,\n    pub workspace: Option<std::path::PathBuf>,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Helper implementations\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nimpl From<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>> for SystemTime {\n    fn from(dt: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>) -> Self {\n        let timestamp_nanos = dt.timestamp_nanos_opt().unwrap_or(0);\n        let duration = std::time::Duration::from_nanos(\n            timestamp_nanos.unsigned_abs() as u64,\n        );\n        if    }\n}\n\n/// A single hook definition: an event filter glob and a dispatch target.\n///\n/// Events whose `event_type` matches the glob are dispatched to the\n/// configured `command` and/or `url`. At least one must be set.\n/// Hooks are fire-and-forget — failures are logged but never block the worker.\n///\n/// # Example\n/// ```yaml\n/// telemetry:\n///   hooks:\n///     - event_filter: \"outcome.*\"\n///       command: \"/path/to/alert.sh\"\n///     - event_filter: \"worker.errored\"\n///       url: \"https://hooks.slack.com/services/...\"\n///     - event_filter: \"effort.recorded\"\n///       command: \"/path/to/cost-tracker.sh\"\n///       url: \"https://dashboard.example.com/ingest\"\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct HookConfig {\n    /// Glob pattern matched against `event_type` (e.g. `\"outcome.*\"`).\n    pub event_filter: String,\n\n    /// Shell command to execute. The event JSON is written to stdin.\n    /// Leave empty or omit when dispatching only to `url`.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub command: String,\n\n    /// HTTP(S) URL to POST the event JSON to (native webhook support).\n    /// `Content-Type: application/json` is set automatically.\n    /// Omit when dispatching only to `command`.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub url: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Telemetry configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TelemetryConfig {\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub file_sink: FileSinkConfig,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub stdout_sink: StdoutSinkConfig,\n    /// Optional hook sinks — dispatch matching events to external commands.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub hooks: Vec<HookConfig>,\n}\n\n/// Health monitoring configuration (heartbeat, peer detection).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct HealthConfig {\n    /// How often to emit a
